learning.whatWillYouLearn: Prerequisites: Arrays, Prefix Sum, Difference Array, Loops, Time Complexity
learning.whyDoesThisExist: Imagine an airline reservation system. A travel agency books 20 seats for flights 2 through 5. Instead of updating every individual flight one by one, the airline records where the booking starts and where it ends. Later, a cumulative calculation determines the total reservations for each flight.
learning.realWorldAnalogy: Imagine an airline reservation system. A travel agency books 20 seats for flights 2 through 5. Instead of updating every individual flight one by one, the airline records where the booking starts and where it ends. Later, a cumulative calculation determines the total reservations for each flight.
learning.whenToUseThis: Use the Difference Array pattern whenever many range updates are followed by queries asking for the final values of every position.
learning.signals: Whenever multiple range additions are followed by a request for the final values of every position, think of the Difference Array pattern.
learning.expectedLearningOutcome: I will know when to use this pattern.

problem.problemStatement: There are n flights labeled from 1 to n. You are given an array bookings where bookings[i] = [first, last, seats] represents a booking that reserves seats seats for every flight from first to last (inclusive). Return an array answer of length n, where answer[i] is the total number of seats reserved for flight i + 1.

examples.explanation: Each booking adds seats across a range of flights.
Flight 2 receives seats from both bookings.

thinkingProcess.naiveIdea: For each booking, iterate through every affected flight and add the booked seats.
thinkingProcess.keyInsight: Each booking affects an entire range of flights. Rather than updating every flight in that range, record only where the booking begins and where it ends. A prefix sum reconstructs the final seat count for every flight.
thinkingProcess.ahaMoment: Each booking affects only its start and end positions in the Difference Array.
thinkingProcess.patternRecognition: Whenever multiple range additions are followed by a request for the final values of every position, think of the Difference Array pattern.
thinkingProcess.whyItWorks: Each booking performs only two updates regardless of the number of affected flights. A single Prefix Sum reconstructs the final reservations for every flight.
thinkingProcess.howToRecognizeSimilar: Whenever multiple range additions are followed by a request for the final values of every position, think of the Difference Array pattern.
commonWrongPaths: Subtracting seats at the wrong index.
Missing the boundary check for last flight.

hints: Think about recording changes instead of updating every flight.
Recover the final answer using Prefix Sum.
commonMisconceptions: Subtracting seats at the wrong index.
Missing the boundary check for last flight.

pattern.name: Difference Array
pattern.recognitionSignals: Whenever multiple range additions are followed by a request for the final values of every position, think of the Difference Array pattern.

approaches.title: Update Every Flight
approaches.idea: For each booking, iterate through every affected flight and add the booked seats.
algorithm: Create an answer array initialized to zero.

approaches.title: Difference Array
approaches.idea: Record only where each booking starts and where it ends. Use a Prefix Sum to reconstruct the reserved seats for every flight.

code.java: Uses Difference Array and Prefix Sum.

approaches.title: Difference Array + Prefix Sum
approaches.idea: Create a Difference Array of size n. For every booking, add seats at the starting flight and subtract seats immediately after the ending flight (if it exists). Finally, compute the Prefix Sum to obtain the total seats reserved for every flight.
approaches.intuition: Each booking performs only two updates regardless of the number of affected flights. A single Prefix Sum reconstructs the final reservations for every flight.
algorithm: Create difference array of size n.
Subtract seats at last if within bounds.

visualization.description: Record only where bookings begin and end.
steps: Booking ends → Remove seats after end.

interviewFollowUps: How would you solve this if flights were added dynamically?
Can this be solved using a Segment Tree?
What if bookings were removed later?
How does this compare with Range Addition?
What if queries were online instead of offline?
interviewTips: Explain why updating every flight is inefficient.
Describe the Difference Array concept clearly.
Mention that Prefix Sum reconstructs the final array.

cheatSheet.keyObservation: Each booking affects only its start and end positions in the Difference Array.
cheatSheet.algorithm: Difference Array → Prefix Sum → Final Reservations.

commonMistakes: Subtracting seats at the wrong index.
Missing the boundary check for last flight.
